name: empty_bbox_placement

symptoms: EVERY motion plan fails ('did not converge') for a robot that loads and looks fine
an object/robot is placed at an absurd pose (Z=-inf or FLT_MAX coordinates)
world poses of robot links read back as +-3.4028e38 / inf
trace_evidence: grasp targets fed to the planner have position components == FLT_MAX (3.4028e38)
the placement sampler computed Z from compute_bbox(robot).min which returned an EMPTY box (min=+FLT_MAX)

two compounding causes: the robot handle's .prim is the ArticulationRoot JOINT prim (its siblings, not children, hold the meshes) AND the URDF-imported visual meshes are invisible instance proxies that ComputeWorldBound(default) skips

when_to_apply: Any pipeline that derives placement/collision geometry from USD bounding boxes of URDF-imported articulated robots (Isaac Sim + omni URDF importer).
repair_strategy: Make the bbox computation robust: if the fast Imageable.ComputeWorldBound path returns an empty range, (a) union each descendant Mesh's local extent/points directly (ignores visibility), (b) traverse INSTANCE PROXIES (Usd.PrimRange(root, Usd.TraverseInstanceProxies(...))), and (c) walk UP ancestor prims until geometry is found (root_joint -> the robot root Xform). Then validate: the robot must land on the floor, not at -inf.
code_sketch: if n and isfinite(gmin): return Range3d(gmin, gmax)
validated_on: InternDataEngine A_on_B_region_sampler: robot Z -inf -> -0.006 (on floor); all 534 'Plan did not converge' failures resolved into real, valid grasp targets
